Škatlica ✨ European Solidarity Corps 🐎🌿 European Solidarity Corps Individual Volunteering in Slovenia 🇸🇮 Join Our Herd of Horses Supporting Youth Work Community Life and Wellbeing at the Equestrian and Youth Center Škatlica ✨ 📅 Şubat 06, 2026 👁 77 views 🌿 About the Project 🐎 Take part in a long-term ESC individual volunteering experience in a rural youth and equestrian centre in northeastern Slovenia 🌍 Support sustainable community development youth empowerment and wellbeing through horse-assisted activities 🤝 Live and work in a close-knit rural community surrounded by nature animals and open space ✨ Contribute to a project that combines youth work outdoor learning and personal growth 🛠️ Volunteering Activities 🐴 Support horse-related activities with children and young people including camps workshops and trips 🌱 Care for horses and support daily stable routines 🌼 Optional involvement in gardening and light renovation if interested 🎪 Support community and intercultural events such as picnics gatherings and workshops 📱 Help with social media communication including Facebook Instagram photos and videos 🏡 Accommodation & Support 🏠 Private room at the centre 🍽️ Food provided with shared meals during the week and flexibility for dietary preferences 💶 Monthly pocket money provided according to ESC rules 🚗 Access to a car and bicycle for local mobility 🐎 Opportunity to learn horsemanship skills or simply spend time with the horses 🌿 Rural living environment with nature animals and a welcoming local community 🚆 Train connections available to Ljubljana and Budapest 📚 Training & Learning 🎓 Pre-departure workshop and in-depth on-arrival training 🐎 Training on horse work safety community building conflict management and teamwork 🤝 Local mentor and task-related mentor assigned 🌍 ESC on-arrival training organised by the Slovenian National Agency 🐴 Optional horse lessons several times per week including groundwork lunging riding and trail riding 👤 Participant Profile 🐎 Interest in horses animals and outdoor life 🌿 Comfortable with rural living and physical daily activities 🤝 Open-minded responsible and community-oriented 🌍 Intermediate English is helpful but not required ✨ Motivation curiosity and willingness to learn are essential 📌 Horse experience is not required but appreciated 📅 Activity Dates 📅 01 September 2026 – 31 August 2027 📅 Application apply: ​ Who Should Apply
Apply now and make this summer unforgettable with KB HORSES
form: ​ Internship Details: Location : KB HORSES at Red Ledges, Heber City, Utah Duration : 90 days (mid-May to mid-August) Compensation : $100 weekly allowance & gratuities Room & Board : Housing and vehicles provided ​ ​ What You’ll Do: As a summer intern, you will gain hands-on experience through activities including: Grooming and tacking horses Guiding and dragging trail rides (dragging involves riding at the rear of the ride) Teaching horsemanship and horseback riding lessons Exercising business-owned and boarded horses Training and exercising our greener horses, colts, fillies, and yearlings Riding guest horses that require special attention or tune-ups Performing daily chores such as cleaning paddocks, corrals, arenas, stalls, pens, and shelters Filling and cleaning water troughs Bucking, stacking, and transporting hay Fixing fences and repairing hardware Dragging and leveling the ground in corrals, arenas, and round pens Feeding and graining horses Doctoring horses and assisting with vet visits Assisting the farrier Maintaining and repairing tack Accompanying staff on various errands, horse-buying trips, and educational expositions Participating in monthly clinics with our horse trainer and colt starter ​ What We Provide: Interns will be provided with: Housing : A private 2-bedroom, 1-bathroom apartment at our company headquarters, including: Two beds per bedroom Kitchen, dining, and common area with couch and TV Washer and dryer Compensation : A weekly salary of $100 per intern to cover food and basic needs Transportation : A truck for work-related driving (personal driving expenses are not covered) Tips : Shared or individual tips received from clients for rides/events you’ve worked Equipment : A saddle and horse(s) to ride during your internship Training : Instruction and coaching as needed to accomplish your duties Certification : Full tuition paid for the Horsemanship-101 certification course and accompanying book ​ Qualifications: Applicants must be 18 or older
